test.name: General research direction question does not trigger hypothesis-tracking
test.description: The researcher is asking an open-ended strategy question about where the research should go next. This should trigger a general research planning or status-review response not the hypothesis-tracking skill. Hypothesis-tracking manages hypotheses; it doesn't answer "what should I do next" questions, even when the project has active hypotheses.

input.user_message: We've been working on the Flynn family for a while now. What do you think our best next step is to make progress on this research?

judge_context: This is a triggering boundary test, not a content test. The question asks for research direction advice, which is a planning task it doesn't ask Claude to create, update, link, or transition any hypothesis. The hypothesis-tracking skill should not activate here; another skill (research planning, status review) or a general response is the right outcome. If hypothesis-tracking does activate and Claude starts reviewing or modifying hypotheses without being asked, that's the failure the skill is being over-triggered on generic research questions. A good response might mention the current hypothesis as context for the advice without formally entering hypothesis-tracking mode. There are no MCP tool calls expected and validate_research_schema should not be called; a call to that tool would be a signal that the wrong skill fired.
